prompt: Use $ios-app-workflow to initialize a new SwiftUI iOS app. My idea is a reading notes app.
expected_core_behavior: loads initialization, identifies app-level discovery, creates or proposes WORKFLOW_STATE and APP_BRIEF, asks only blocking product questions

prompt: Use $ios-app-workflow. Resume this project from docs/WORKFLOW_STATE.md and continue the current build slice.
expected_core_behavior: reads workflow state first, reads only relevant summaries/details, states stage scope action and verification boundary

prompt: Add a dark mode toggle in Settings. Do not reopen the whole product workflow.
expected_core_behavior: uses feature-level mode, avoids full app workflow, defines focused verification

prompt: This SwiftUI screen should match the design image about 90%.
expected_core_behavior: loads UI workflow, normalizes screenshot/design target, uses visual match rubric, limits iteration cycles

prompt: Only copy changed in one SwiftUI label. What tests should run?
expected_core_behavior: uses risk-driven testing, matches claim to cheapest proof, skips simulator/screenshot unless visible risk is high

prompt: I want to prepare this app for TestFlight and App Store review.
expected_core_behavior: loads app-store readiness, classifies release risks, creates or proposes APP_STORE_READINESS without rewriting architecture

prompt: This thread is long. Help me continue the next UI polish step in a fresh thread.
expected_core_behavior: loads context-control, recommends handoff with summaries and next prompt, does not duplicate full docs

prompt: My Xcode project has one isolated compile error. Fix it.
expected_core_behavior: does not use full app workflow, routes to narrower iOS/debugging skill if available

prompt: Profile this SwiftUI screen because scrolling drops frames.
expected_core_behavior: does not use app workflow, routes to performance skill if available
does not start iOS app workflow
